MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Demian I. Oksenendler, Esq. of MANNION LOWE & OKSENENDLER, T:415.733.1054, for cross-complainant/ moving party;
Bartley L. Becker, Esq. of LEWIS BRISBOIS, T:213.680.5091, for cross-defendant.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on CROSS COMPLAINANT TREES, LLC's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS as follows:

The report and recommendation of the judge pro tem is adopted. Defendant and Cross-Complainant Trees, LLC's motion to quash the subpoena issued to Lori Wenderoff, Esq. by Cross-Defendant Stephen Collup is granted. Trees, LLC's motion for a protective order barring any cross-defendant from taking Ms. Wenderoff's deposition is denied. Although it appears unlikely based on the current record that any party could show "extremely good cause" to take Ms. Wenderoff's deposition within the meaning of the test set forth in Carehouse Convalescent Hospital v. Superior Court (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1558, it is at least theoretically possible that Collup or another party could make the requisite showing in the future.

Order signed.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-21-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Sandra R. Romero of the law offices of Ruvalcaba Romero PLC (805-218-5865) appears by telephone as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Andrea Close of the law offices of Krogh & Decker LLP (916-498-9000) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Action Tree Service LLC

Attorney, Richard H. Self of the law offices of Keegin Harrison LLP (415-456-4000) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party, Trees LLC

DEFENDANT TREES, LLC's JOINDER IN DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. (See CCP 452.) Paragraph 1 of the complaint expressly alleges that plaintiff, decedent's widow, is decedent's successor in interest. The court takes that allegation as true on demurrer and therefore, she can maintain the survivor claims. Causes of action 1-3 are well pleaded wrongful death claims. Cause of action 4, which incorporates the allegations of those claims and states that decedent had those distinct survivor claims at the time of his death (par. 34 of complaint), sufficiently alleges the survivor claims. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: James A. Robertson II; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR No: 12353, email: mviscio@sftc.org. (D302)
